zh.lifeSummary: 當William De Clifford於1270年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 奧 澤福時，他的父親William De Clifford35歲，母親Maud Seymour24歲。他於1309年與Katherine de Maulton在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 白 希雷斯特結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1321年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 奧 澤福享年51歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When William De Clifford was born in 1270, in Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, England, his father, William De Clifford, was 35 and his mother, Maud Seymour, was 24. He married Katherine de Maulton in 1309, in Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1321, in Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 51.

ko.Frampton, Gloucestershire, , England: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터
ko.lifeSummary: William De Clifford(이)가 1270년 영국 그루스스터실르 프램프턴  세버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William De Clifford(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Seymour(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1309년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터에서 Katherine de Maulton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1321년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴  세버에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William De Clifford nasceu em 1270, em Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William De Clifford, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Maud Seymour, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Katherine de Maulton em 1309, em Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1321, em Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William De Clifford родился в 1270 г. в местности фрамптон он сивирн, глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени William De Clifford было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Seymour, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine de Maulton в 1309 г. в местности фрамптон би сиренсестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1321 г. в местности фрамптон он сивирн, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William De Clifford nació en 1270, en Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, William De Clifford, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Maud Seymour, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Katherine de Maulton en 1309, en Frampton by Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1321, en Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 51 años.
